title: fraxinus angustifolia var. rostrata
intro: Fraxinus angustifolia var. rostrata, also known as the narrow-leaved ash, is a variety of the European ash tree that is native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which have been used to treat various health conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The narrow-leaved ash has been found to have several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting immune function. In herbal preparations, the bark and leaves of the tree are typically used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions that can be used to promote overall health and well-being.
